summary: The session focused on the success of albums on the Billboard 200 chart, specifically discussing Taylor Swift's "Lover" and Billie Eilish's "When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?" The conversation also explored the impact of social media on the music industry and trends in music genres.
facts: Taylor Swift's album "Lover" performed well on the Billboard 200 chart due to her large fanbase, extensive promotion, and strategic release timing amid her feud with her former record label. Billie Eilish's album "When We All Fall Asleep, Where Do We Go?" achieved success through her unique sound, strategic promotion, and the popularity of the lead single "bad guy." Social media has changed the music industry by allowing artists to connect directly with fans and gain exposure without traditional gatekeepers. Trends in music include the fusion of genres, an emphasis on authenticity in songwriting, and a resurgence of retro sounds from the 70s, 80s, and 90s. Up-and-coming musicians recommended include Clairo, Dominic Fike, Arlo Parks, Remi Wolf, Girl in Red, Beabadoobee, Celeste, Omar Apollo, Baby Keem, and Omar Velasco.
